summary.intent: Fix pass-6 Codex review findings (RUa, RUe, RUg, RUh) for the file_upload component: edge cases from a prior pass-5 change, a keyboard focusability gap, and a disputed finding about OpenFilePicker guard ordering.
summary.outcome: RUa (reconciled_state handles DragOver+uploading), RUe (SetProps clears drag flags during Uploading+dragging), and RUg (item_attrs adds tabindex='0') were implemented and tested. RUh was proven incorrect by code inspection and a falsifying test. 143 tests pass, spec updated, committed and pushed.

repo: The file_upload component lives in crates/ars-components/src/specialized/file_upload/ with mod.rs (impl) and tests.rs (tests) as separate files.
Spec lives at spec/components/specialized/file-upload.md and must pass a spec validate gate before committing.
The disabled/readonly guard in the main match arm (around L871) covers all message variants including OpenFilePicker (L1084) via a catch-all `_ => None`, making RUh's premise factually wrong.
Item snapshots are stored and must be manually accepted when attributes change (e.g., adding tabindex='0' changed 5 item snapshots).
llvm-cov branch coverage is unreliable with stale profraws; must clean llvm-cov state before measuring to get accurate numbers (stale gave 87.80%, clean gave 90.03%).

code.finding: Disabled/readonly guard catch-all `_ => None` — covers all message variants including OpenFilePicker, so no special-casing needed for inert state.
OpenFilePicker arm is inside the main match, after the disabled guard, so it is already blocked when component is inert.
rejection_message() has an empty/non-empty branch that was the last uncovered branch; covered by adding a two-sided test.

workflow: Codex review findings should be individually verified against source before implementing — RUh was a false positive caught by reading the code.
When llvm-cov reports a suspicious coverage drop (e.g., file going from ~99% to 52%), suspect stale profraw files and clean before re-measuring.
Markdown table alignment linting (MD060) is a non-gated cosmetic nit; GitHub renders misaligned tables fine and manual re-padding is error-prone — safe to skip.
Item snapshot diffs should be confirmed to contain only the expected attribute change before bulk-accepting to avoid masking regressions.

friction: Stale llvm-cov profraw files caused a misleading coverage drop from ~90% to 87.80% (and file_upload appearing at 52.4%), requiring a clean + re-measure cycle.
Multiple spec edit passes were needed to keep the §3.1 ARIA table column widths within bounds without triggering MD060 linting errors.
The §2 anatomy table row for Item widened after the tabindex addition, leaving alignment cosmetically off — accepted as a known nit rather than fixing.
open_items: RUh (OpenFilePicker guard) has a falsifying test committed, but the Codex finding thread (finding-6) was still open at end of pass — may need a follow-up response to close it.
MD060 table alignment nit in §2 anatomy of spec/components/specialized/file-upload.md is unresolved (intentionally deferred as non-gated cosmetic).
